intitule: Present Simple vs Present Continuous - Form and Use

enonce.texte: Exercise 1: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verbs in brackets. Use either the Present Simple or the Present Continuous.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct tense.
questions.reponse: 1. is raining; 2. go; 3. is studying; 4. watches; 5. are playing; 6. speaks; 7. is working; 8. get up; 9. is cooking; 10. writes.

sousQuestions.texte: Listen! It ______ (rain) outside.
I usually ______ (go) to school by bus.
My sister ______ (study) for her exam right now.
Every evening, my father ______ (watch) the news on TV.
The children ______ (play) football in the garden at the moment.
My teacher ______ (speak) English very well.
Be quiet! The baby ______ (sleep) in the next room.
We always ______ (get up) early during the week.
My mother ______ (cook) dinner in the kitchen now.
She ______ (write) letters to her friends every month.

intitule: Distinguishing Present Simple and Present Continuous

enonce.texte: Exercise 2: Choose the correct tense. Explain why you made your choice.

questions.texte: Select the correct option and justify your answer.
questions.reponse: 1. are you doing (action in progress); 2. tastes (state verb); 3. am waiting (action in progress); 4. walks (habit); 5. is having (action in progress); 6. knows (state verb); 7. are you laughing (action in progress); 8. loves (state verb); 9. am studying (action in progress); 10. rains (general truth).

sousQuestions.texte: What ______ (you / do) right now?
This soup ______ (taste) delicious!
I ______ (wait) for the bus at the moment.
My grandmother always ______ (walk) to the market.
She ______ (have) a bath right now.
He ______ (know) the answer to the question.
Why ______ (you / laugh) at me?
My little sister ______ (love) chocolate.
I ______ (study) for my exam these days.
It usually ______ (rain) a lot in this region.

enonce.texte: Exercise 3: Some verbs are not normally used in the continuous form. Complete the sentences using the correct tense. Identify the stative verbs.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks. Be careful with stative verbs.
questions.reponse: 1. think (opinion); 2. smell (action); 3. believe (state); 4. is looking (action); 5. want (state); 6. are you thinking (opinion); 7. feel (state); 8. is tasting (action); 9. prefer (state); 10. is weighing (action).

sousQuestions.texte: I ______ (think) that you are right.
The chef ______ (smell) the soup to check if it's ready.
I ______ (believe) in equal rights for everyone.
She ______ (look) at the beautiful painting right now.
My children ______ (want) to visit the museum.
What ______ (you / think) about this idea?
I ______ (feel) that we should help them.
The waiter ______ (taste) the wine.
I ______ (prefer) tea to coffee.
The vendor ______ (weigh) the fruits on the scale.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Read the text and answer the questions below.

My name is Peter. I am a student at the University of Yaoundé. I get up at 6:00 AM every day. I take a shower and have breakfast. Then I go to school by taxi. In the morning, I attend lectures and take notes. Right now, I am sitting in the library and reading my notes. My friend John is studying in the laboratory. Usually, we have lunch together at the campus restaurant. At the moment, my phone is ringing. I am not answering it because I am busy studying.

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the text.
questions.reponse: 1. Peter is a student at the University of Yaoundé. 2. He gets up at 6:00 AM. 3. He goes to school by taxi. 4. He is sitting in the library. 5. John is studying in the laboratory. 6. They usually have lunch at the campus restaurant. 7. He is not answering the phone because he is busy studying.

sousQuestions.texte: Who is Peter?
sousQuestions.reponse: He is a student at the University of Yaoundé.

sousQuestions.texte: What time does Peter get up every day?
How does Peter go to school?
Where is Peter sitting right now?
sousQuestions.reponse: He is sitting in the library.

sousQuestions.texte: What is John doing at the moment?
sousQuestions.reponse: He is studying in the laboratory.

sousQuestions.texte: Where do they usually have lunch?
Why is Peter not answering his phone?

intitule: Analyzing Habits vs. Actions in Progress

enonce.texte: Exercise 5: Analyze the following sentences. Identify the habits, general truths, and actions in progress. Explain why you made your choice.

questions.texte: For each sentence, state the tense and explain the reason for using that tense.
questions.reponse: 1. Present Simple (habit); 2. Present Continuous (action in progress); 3. Present Simple (general truth); 4. Present Continuous (action in progress); 5. Present Continuous (action in progress); 6. Present Simple (habit); 7. Present Continuous (action in progress); 8. Present Simple (general truth).

sousQuestions.texte: My mother always wakes up at 5 AM to prepare breakfast.
Look! The children are playing in the park.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius.
I am reading a book about African history.
She is working on a new project these days.
We visit our grandparents every weekend.
My friend is learning to drive a car at the moment.
The Earth revolves around the Sun.

enonce.texte: Exercise 6: Write a paragraph of about 100 words describing your daily routine (habits) and what you are doing right now (actions in progress). Use both the Present Simple and the Present Continuous correctly.

questions.texte: Write a descriptive paragraph using both tenses.
questions.reponse: Indicative answer. Example: I am a student in Première at the Lycée de Bonamoussadi. I usually get up at 5:30 AM. I take a shower and have breakfast with my family. I go to school by bus at 6:45 AM. At school, I attend classes and take notes. Right now, I am sitting in my classroom and I am listening to my teacher. My classmates are writing their exercises. We are all working hard.

sousQuestions.texte: Write about your habits.
sousQuestions.reponse: I usually get up at 5:30 AM. I go to school by bus. I attend classes and take notes.

sousQuestions.texte: Write about what you are doing right now.
sousQuestions.reponse: Right now, I am sitting in my classroom. I am listening to my teacher. My classmates are writing their exercises.

sousQuestions.texte: Organize your paragraph logically.
sousQuestions.reponse: The paragraph should have a clear structure: introduction (who you are), habits (using Present Simple), and current actions (using Present Continuous).

intitule: Interview - Asking about Habits and Actions

enonce.texte: Exercise 7: Create a dialogue between two people. One person asks questions about habits and current actions. The other person answers. Use both tenses.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue with at least 10 exchanges.
questions.reponse: Indicative answer. Example: A: Good morning! What do you usually do on weekends? B: I usually visit my cousins. A: What are you doing this weekend? B: I am going to the market with my mother. A: Do you like reading? B: Yes, I read a lot. A: What book are you reading now? B: I am reading a novel by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie.

sousQuestions.texte: Use at least 5 questions about habits.
sousQuestions.reponse: What do you do in the morning? Do you go to school by bus? Do you like watching TV? What do you eat for breakfast? Do you do your homework in the evening?

sousQuestions.texte: Use at least 5 questions about actions in progress.
sousQuestions.reponse: What are you doing right now? Are you reading a book? What are you studying today? Are you working on a project? What is your friend doing?

sousQuestions.texte: Write the responses correctly in the appropriate tense.
sousQuestions.reponse: I usually go to school by bus. I am reading a novel right now.
